description: Dense relational corpus designed to stress graph traversal. Recurring entities (Theo, Priya, Lucia, Kim, Dani, Mango, Sangam, Crux, Datadog, Anthropic) appear across many memories so spreading activation has paths cosine cannot find directly.
persona: Alex Rivera — staff designer, Austin then SF, partner Dani, dog Mango, sister Lucia in Mexico City

turns.content: I'm Alex Rivera, just turned 32. I work at Datadog as a staff designer in Austin.
My partner Dani moved here with me from Brooklyn last year. She's an architect at HKS.
We have a 4-year-old papillon named Mango. He's tiny but thinks he's a great dane.
We live in the East Austin neighborhood, walking distance to Mueller park.
I usually start my day with a 30-minute walk with Mango at Mueller Lake Park.
Dani works on healthcare facility design — hospitals, clinics, that kind of work.
I've been at Datadog for three years, mostly working on the observability dashboard.
Before Datadog I was at Stripe, but design at Stripe felt too constrained.
My best friend Theo works at SpaceX. We've known each other since college at UT Austin.
Theo and I climb every Tuesday at Crux Climbing Gym on Burnet Road in Austin.
Theo lives in Hyde Park with his partner Priya. They got married last year.
Priya is a chef — she runs a small restaurant called Sangam in East Austin.
Sangam serves modern Indian food, mostly small plates and natural wines.
I try to climb V4 problems but Theo just sent his first V8 last month.
Crux just expanded with an outdoor section — bouldering walls in their parking lot.
Theo and I want to do an outdoor trip to Hueco Tanks this winter for real climbing.
My sister Lucia lives in Mexico City — Roma Norte specifically.
Lucia teaches comparative literature at UNAM. She's been there four years.
My mom moved to Mexico City two years ago to be closer to Lucia.
Mom lives in a small apartment in Coyoacán, about 20 minutes from Lucia.
My dad still lives in Portland, Oregon — he never wanted to leave the Pacific Northwest.
Dad has a small woodworking shop in his garage in Portland.
I FaceTime my mom every Sunday afternoon, has been our routine since I moved out.
Had dinner at Sangam last night — Priya cooked us off-menu mole-spiced quail.
Sangam was named after the confluence of three rivers in Indian mythology.
Priya trained at Junoon in New York before moving to Austin to be with Theo.
Theo's birthday is December 12th — same week as my anniversary with Dani.
We're throwing Theo a surprise dinner at Sangam, Priya is closing the restaurant for it.
Theo's parents flew in from Mumbai for the surprise — we have to keep it tight.
Priya makes the best mezcal cocktails in town. She uses Vago Elote.
Started a pottery class at Castle Hill Pottery Studio. Twice a week, evenings.
Kim is in my pottery class — she's a pediatrician at Dell Children's Hospital.
Kim and I are now friends outside class, we get tacos at Veracruz on Cesar Chavez.
Veracruz al Pastor are the best migas tacos in Austin, hands down.
My pottery is mostly bad but I'm getting better at centering. Kim's pieces are gorgeous.
Kim recommended a vet for Mango — Dr. Patel at Cherrywood Animal Hospital.
Flying to Mexico City tomorrow with Dani for Christmas with mom and Lucia.
Lucia's apartment in Roma Norte is hosting the family — mom, me, Dani, and Lucia.
Mom made tamales for Nochebuena — same recipe for thirty years.
Lucia's neighborhood has the best cantinas — Salón Tenampa is our usual.
Took mom to see the Frida Kahlo museum in Coyoacán, near her apartment.
Bought Dani a black clay olla from a small shop in San Ángel for her cooking.
Took Mango to Dr. Patel today at Cherrywood — annual checkup, all clear.
Dr. Patel said Mango's a perfect papillon weight, could maybe lose 200g.
Kim invited us to her place for dinner Saturday — she lives in Bouldin Creek.
Kim's making lasagna — apparently her one non-pediatric specialty.
Kim recommended I check out the Tomás García pottery exhibit at Cantanker.
Dr. Patel also recommended a dog trainer in Mueller for Mango's pulling.
Lucia is visiting next week — staying with us for five days.
I started collecting mezcal — currently have 14 bottles, mostly from Oaxaca.
My favorite is a Tobalá from Real Minero, gift from Lucia last Christmas.
Lucia teaches a seminar on Borges every spring at UNAM — it's wildly popular.
Lucia's coming with us to Sangam — Priya is making her a special tasting menu.
Theo and Priya are joining us for Lucia's farewell dinner at Sangam.
I'm leaving Datadog — got an offer from Anthropic in San Francisco.
It's a senior product designer role on the Claude consumer team. I start in May.
Dani is supportive but worried about leaving the Austin design community.
Mom is heartbroken — said now both her kids are far from home.
Theo says I should commit even though it means leaving climbing partner status.
Anthropic is reimbursing the move and giving us a temp housing stipend for 60 days.
We moved to San Francisco last week — Mango is freaked out by the buses.
We're in the Mission District, two blocks from Dolores Park.
Dani found a freelance contract with a small SF firm called Studio Terpeluk.
She's working on a clinic project in Bayview — back in healthcare design.
Anthropic's office is in SoMa, I take the J train every weekday morning.
Mango's new vet is Dr. Lin at SF Pet Hospital — Kim helped me find her.
Found a great climbing gym in SF — Dogpatch Boulders is my new home gym.
I miss Crux but Dogpatch has a stronger bouldering scene, more competitions.
Started pottery again at Sharon Art Studio in Golden Gate Park — beginner studio.
The teacher there knew Tomás García personally, small world stuff.
Dani and I do a long walk every Sunday at Lands End — Mango on his long lead.
I'm doing 20km on the bike most weekdays, mainly along the Embarcadero.
Flying to Austin Friday — first trip back since the move. Three days.
Theo organized a climb day at Crux for old times. Maybe my last V4 there.
Dinner Saturday is at Sangam — Priya is doing a ten-course tasting for us.
Priya is opening a second Sangam location in SoMa — incredible coincidence.
She'll be in SF every few weeks during buildout. Theo says he'll come too.
Visited Kim at her new house in Tarrytown — she upgraded from Bouldin Creek.
Kim showed me her newest piece — a celadon vase, she's been working on it for months.
Took Mango to Mueller Lake Park one more time before flying home — full circle.
